commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: The component fails to render the required 5-day forecast strip and weather condition icon because the sub-components 'ForecastItem' and 'WeatherMetrics' are referenced in 'index.tsx' but not defined or imported in that file.
issues.fix: Implement the 'ForecastItem' and 'WeatherMetrics' components in their respective files and import them correctly into 'index.tsx'.

issues.description: The provided code contains redundant and orphan 'MainView' implementations in 'ForecastItem.tsx' and 'WeatherMetrics.tsx' which are never used. 'index.tsx' attempts to use sub-components that don't exist in its scope.
issues.fix: Remove unused files and implement the sub-components correctly as modular exports, then import them into the main index view.

issues.description: The current layout does not clearly map to the requested visual hierarchy of a weather card, and the missing icons for weather conditions make it look incomplete.
issues.fix: Utilize the 'WEATHER_ICONS' mapping from constants to display the appropriate emoji/icon based on the condition prop.

evaluations.critique: The project is currently broken because the main component references sub-components that are not properly exported or integrated. While the design intent is visible, the execution is fragmented across multiple 'MainView' files that contain redundant code instead of modular sub-components.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: Category color logic is inconsistent across components. ElementCell uses a local hardcoded map, CategoryLegend uses another local map, and the constants file provides a third set of definitions that is currently unused by the UI components.
issues.fix: Centralize all color logic in the constants file and import these functions into both ElementCell and CategoryLegend.

issues.description: The ElementCell has fixed dimensions (100x100px), which may cause overflow or layout issues on smaller screens. Grid cell behavior is brittle with hardcoded pixel values.
issues.fix: Use flexible units or aspect-ratio CSS properties to allow the grid to respond to container width.

issues.description: Interactive elements (ElementCell) lack focus states, making keyboard navigation difficult for users relying on visual focus indicators. The DetailPanel is not dynamically announced via ARIA live regions.
issues.fix: Add explicit :focus-visible styles to cells and wrap the DetailPanel in an aria-live region.

issues.description: Redundant logic inside components; ElementCell and CategoryLegend both define local maps instead of consuming the provided utility functions in constants.ts.
issues.fix: Import getCategoryColor from /virtual/constants.ts in both sub-components.

evaluations.critique: The component structure is logically sound and the use of CSS Grid for the table layout is excellent. However, the maintenance is hindered by fragmented color logic, which prevents the legend from accurately matching the grid cells. Cleaning up the state/constant synchronization will make this production-ready.

issues.description: Category color logic is inconsistent across components. ElementCell uses a local hardcoded map, CategoryLegend uses another local map, and the constants file provides a third set of definitions that is currently unused by the UI components.
issues.fix: Centralize all color logic in the constants file and import these functions into both ElementCell and CategoryLegend.

issues.fix: Use flexible units or aspect-ratio CSS properties to allow the grid to respond to container width.

Cleaning up the state/constant synchronization will make this production-ready.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

issues.description: The code provided is truncated in the main entry point (components/index.tsx), missing the rendering logic for steps 2 and 3 and the submission UI.
issues.fix: Complete the implementation of the main switch case in the component body and ensure the rendering of ProfileForm, PreferencesForm, and ReviewSection.

issues.description: Step Indicator logic is confusing; user requested 1/2/3, but code uses 0-based indexing leading to 'Step 0 of 3' or 'Step 1 of 3' visual mismatch.
issues.fix: Normalize currentStep to be 1-based for the UI components and internal indexing logic.

issues.description: The 'Next' button does not explicitly display its disabled state via visual feedback (e.g., opacity or cursor styling), and there are no transition animations between steps.
issues.fix: Bind the 'disabled' prop of the button to the 'isNextDisabled' state from the hook.

evaluations.critique: The component structure is well-modularized and follows the design system patterns accurately. However, the final file is cut off, rendering the component non-functional. The logic for indexing the onboarding steps needs a cleaner abstraction to avoid off-by-one errors in the UI.

issues.fix: Complete the implementation of the main switch case in the component body and ensure the rendering of ProfileForm, PreferencesForm, and ReviewSection.

issues.description: Step Indicator logic is confusing; user requested 1/2/3, but code uses 0-based indexing leading to 'Step 0 of 3' or 'Step 1 of 3' visual mismatch.
issues.fix: Normalize currentStep to be 1-based for the UI components and internal indexing logic.

issues.description: The 'Next' button does not explicitly display its disabled state via visual feedback (e.g., opacity or cursor styling), and there are no transition animations between steps.
issues.fix: Bind the 'disabled' prop of the button to the 'isNextDisabled' state from the hook.
